fr:title.text: Two-dimensional Kripke Semantics II: Stability and Completeness

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Two-dimensional Kripke Semantics II: Stability and Completeness

fr:meta.name: venue

fr:link.title: MFPS ’24: 40th International Conference on Mathematical Foundations of Programming Semantics

fr:meta.fr:link: MFPS ’24: 40th International Conference on Mathematical Foundations of Programming Semantics

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: We revisit the duality between Kripke and algebraic semantics of intuitionistic and intuitionistic modal logic. We find that there is a certain mismatch between the two semantics, which means that not all algebraic models can be embedded into a Kripke model. This leads to an alternative proposal for a relational semantics, the stable semantics. Instead of an arbitrary partial order, the stable semantics requires a distributive lattice of worlds. We constructively show that the stable semantics is exactly as complete as the algebraic semantics. Categorifying these results leads to a 2-duality between two-dimensional stable semantics and categories of product-preserving presheaves, i.e. models of algebraic theories in the style of Lawvere.

The study of modal logic has witnessed tremendous development following the introduction of Kripke semantics. However, recent developments in programming languages and type theory have led to a second way of studying modalities, namely through their categorical semantics. We show how the two correspond.

fr:title.text: MFPS ’24: 40th International Conference on Mathematical Foundations of Programming Semantics

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: MFPS ’24: 40th International Conference on Mathematical Foundations of Programming Semantics
